Package org.eventb.internal.core.typecheck

Class TypeCheckResult

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.AbstractResult
org.eventb.internal.core.typecheck.TypeCheckResult
All Implemented Interfaces:
IResult, ITypeCheckResult
public class TypeCheckResult extends AbstractResult implements ITypeCheckResult
This class implements most of the algorithm of the type checker and provides the result to clients.

Type-checking is implemented in two passes. First, traverse the formula with Formula.typeCheck(TypeCheckResult, BoundIdentDecl[]), creating fresh type variables and registering type equations. Then, attempt to solve all type variables by calling solveTypeVariables(), then traverse again the formula with Formula.solveType(TypeUnifier), storing solved types or null if the type could not be inferred.

  • Constructor Details

    • TypeCheckResult

      public TypeCheckResult(ISealedTypeEnvironment typeEnvironment)
      Constructs the result with the specified initial type environment
      Parameters:
      typeEnvironment - a type environment

  • Method Details

    • getIdentType

      public final Type getIdentType(FreeIdentifier ident)
      Returns the type associated to the given free identifier.

      If the identifier is not registered in any environment, creates a type variable for its type.

      Parameters:
      ident - the free identifier to lookup
      Returns:
      the type associated to the given identifier

    • getInferredEnvironment

      public final IInferredTypeEnvironment getInferredEnvironment()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeCheckResult
      Returns the inferred type environment.

      The inferred type environment contains exactly the types of all identifiers that occur free in the type-checked formula and that are not typed in the initial type environment. Hence, the inferred environment always has an empty intersection with the initial environment.

      In case of success, this inferred type environment can be safely merged within the initial type environment using ITypeEnvironmentBuilder.addAll(ITypeEnvironment).

      Specified by:
      getInferredEnvironment in interface ITypeCheckResult
      Returns:
      the inferred type environment or null if type-check failed

    • getInitialTypeEnvironment

      public final ITypeEnvironment getInitialTypeEnvironment()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeCheckResult
      Returns the initial type environment that was provided to the Formula.typeCheck(ITypeEnvironment) method. This initial environment is never modified by the type-check operation.
      Specified by:
      getInitialTypeEnvironment in interface ITypeCheckResult
      Returns:
      the initial type environment

    • getUnifier

      public final TypeUnifier getUnifier()
      Returns the type unifier associated with this result.
      Returns:
      the type unifier

    • newFreshVariable

      public final TypeVariable newFreshVariable(SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new fresh variable with the corresponding source location. The source location is only specified for terminal type variable, i.e. type variables on terminal nodes.
      Parameters:
      location - the location of the type variable's corresponding symbol
      Returns:
      a type variable

    • solveTypeVariables

      public final void solveTypeVariables()
      Solves the type variables.getTypeEnvironment

      Every type variable created during this type-check will be solved to a fully solved type (that is a type that do not contain any type variable). If the type variable can not be solved, it adds the corresponding problem and makes this result fail.

    • unify

      public final <T extends Formula<?>> void unify(Type left, Type right, T origin)
      Adds the specified type equation (left ≣ right) in the type equation list and unifies it with the existing type equations. A problem is added to the problem list if this equation tries to insert a conflicting type equation.
      Parameters:
      left - the type on the left hand side of the equation
      right - the type on the right hand side of the equation
      origin - the formula that generated this equation

    • getFormulaFactory

      public final FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()

    • addUnificationProblem

      public final <T extends Formula<?>> void addUnificationProblem(Type left, Type right, T origin)

    • analyzeExpression

      public void analyzeExpression(Expression expr)
      Analyze the expression type to check that given sets introduced are present in the initial or inferred environment and otherwise to check their compatibility with existent environments and add them in the inferred environment.
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression for which the type will be checked

    • analyzeType

      public void analyzeType(Type type, Formula<?> source)
      Analyze the type to check that given sets introduced are present in the initial or inferred environment and otherwise to check their compatibility with existent environments and add them in the inferred environment.
      Parameters:
      type - the type that will be checked
      source - the formula from which the type has been extracted
